verdicts.reasoning: Emotional (60%): Strong pass. The panel uses a warm saturated gold/amber palette with cream interior — quintessential Hyrule UI warmth. Rounded corners and decorative jewel/gem ornaments at top-center and corners give a friendly, charming, toy-diorama feel. No grim or dark tones anywhere. Fully consistent with Echoes of Wisdom bright mood. Technical (40%): Pass. Hard 1px outlines are visible and crisp at all zoom levels. The gold border shows clear 2-3 tone hard shading (highlight rim, midtone body, shadow edge). Pixel-grid-snapped construction is evident in the corner bracket details and gem decorations. The ornament sprites are hand-pixeled with good shape clarity. Only minor weakness: the cream text-body interior is a flat fill with no pixel-level texture or inner-shadow detail, which the Denzi peer reference suggests should have more granularity. Does not drop the score below pass threshold.

Silhouette reads clearly at 1x — dual stacked rectangles with rounded corners instantly register as dialog panel. At 4x, pixel art is crisp with clean edges. Color palette is warm amber/gold with cream, evoking friendly classic RPG (Zelda-adjacent) rather than distinctly cute/toy charm. Red gem and blue accent icons add personality and pass sticker test for the title bar. Dark bg: golden frame pops well, strong contrast. Light bg: frame still legible but softens noticeably, the cream lower text box nearly merges with a bright background. Main charm gap: the lower text area is plain beige — no decorative personality, slightly mushy against similar-value backgrounds. The dotted inner border on the lower box is a nice touch but too subtle to rescue it. Overall: clear silhouette, decent charm, but falls short of the toy/sticker-grade cuteness threshold. Passes readability, marginal on charm depth and light-bg contrast.

Panel is dominated by warm amber-gold borders, parchment-cream interiors, and golden-yellow fill — a tight analogous warm palette with high internal harmony. Outlines/shadow edges use dark warm brown (not pure black or grey), satisfying the colored-darks requirement. A small teal-blue gem icon provides a textbook warm-cool complementary accent that energizes without clashing. Red-orange decorative flourishes stay in the warm family. All hues map cleanly to the Echoes reference swatch (amber, parchment gold, teal, red-orange all present). Mood reads BRIGHT and WARM — zero goth, zero cold-corporate, zero neon. Saturation is rich but controlled. No muddy desaturated areas detected. The neutral grey is the sprite-sheet canvas background only and is not part of the panel asset.

The panel_dialog_v01 is immediately locatable against the dark dungeon background — large warm gold/amber border with cream interior creates strong contrast against cool teal-black tiles. Spottable well within 0.5s. Pixel art style (ornate corner face-icons, chunky pixelated border) is consistent with existing game UI (matches amber 攻击 button palette). Both the upper golden section and lower cream section have high tonal contrast with the dungeon floor. No confusion with game entities — clearly reads as a UI overlay panel. Echoes cleanliness is good: warm retro palette, no visual noise. Minor issue: both interior zones are empty (no text content), making it feel placeholder; this does not affect readability but would be a content concern at runtime. Arrow marker position aligns to the right edge of the panel near the 压板 button, but the panel itself is unmistakable. Overall strong integration.

The dialog panel sprite itself (panel_dialog_v01) uses warm golden-yellow and parchment/cream tones with decorative corner ornaments — this palette is coherent with cute fairy-tale aesthetics. However, the surrounding scene context is dark teal/near-black dungeon tiles with deep red accents, which reads as dungeon-crawler rather than bright sunny magical. The panel itself passes tone check (warm golds, parchment, rounded decorative frame), but it floats over a backdrop that conflicts with the target mood. Sprite-in-isolation: 8/10. Sprite-in-scene: 6/10. Averaged to 7 marginal — the panel is on-tone but the scene integration exposes a mood mismatch worth flagging.

issues: panel occupies ~38% of screen width and ~37% of screen height, dominating the game world view and reducing toy-diorama feel
dialog panel is significantly larger than the 32x32 player character visible at left edge, breaking relative scale hierarchy
two-section panel layout creates a wallpaper-like fullscreen overlay impression rather than a small in-world popup
panel decorative corner icons (~30x30px) are at the upper limit for HUD icon budget
verdicts.reasoning: The panel_dialog_v01 composite shows a large dialog panel centered on screen measuring approximately 480x260px against a 1280x690 viewport. As a UI overlay panel this is a different category than world entities, so direct player-size comparison is less critical, but the panel still occupies a substantial portion of the playfield. The top golden section with decorative scroll corners and the bottom cream text area together create a visually heavy overlay. The panel width (~38% of screen) and its vertical positioning over the center of the play area significantly obscures the game world, pushing toward a wallpaper/modal feel rather than a lightweight diorama hint. Corner ornament icons appear around 28-32px which edges past the ≤24px HUD icon guideline. The pixel-art style is consistent with the scene aesthetic and perspective is flat/top-down matching the game. Score 6: style matches but size is marginal-to-large for a diorama feel.

At 8x zoom, multiple AI tells are visible. (1) Gold/orange border transitions through soft gradient blending rather than 2-3 hard pixel tones — characteristic diffusion model output rather than deliberate pixel art shading. (2) Edges are anti-aliased with sub-pixel blending; not crisp single-pixel steps expected of authored pixel art. (3) Scattered pink/red stray pixels visible along the top and right edges of the outer frame — not part of any intentional color. (4) The face medallion icon at upper-left shows mushy/melting detail: internal forms lose pixel-grid alignment at 8x and bleed into surroundings. (5) Bottom panel inner decorative border is geometrically asymmetric — curved notch indentations appear on left/top side but the right side geometry differs, a hallmark of AI spatial confusion. (6) Outline weight varies across the same continuous border segment (1px in places, 2px in others, missing at some corners). Positive: overall semantic structure is coherent (header + body dialog layout), fill areas are relatively clean, color palette is consistent. The issues are enough to flag as marginal; the asymmetric inner border and noise pixels are the clearest hard fails.

Peer sheet is classic Denzi dungeon tileset: cool grey/teal/dark palette, heavy black pixel outlines on every element, high detail density with dithered stone textures, directional shadows, muted color temperature throughout. Candidate panel_dialog_v01 fails on multiple style axes independent of item-type: (1) Color temperature — candidate is warm gold/cream/amber while entire peer set is cool-grey/dark; this is a palette identity failure, not an item-type allowance. (2) Palette overlap — near zero; peers use charcoal, slate, teal, dark crimson; candidate uses gold (#C8A840 range), ivory, warm brown — no shared hues. (3) Outline weight/color — peers uniformly use bold black pixel outlines; candidate substitutes a decorative golden border frame with no black silhouette outline, creating a fundamentally different visual grammar. (4) Detail density — peers are texture-dense with micro-pixel work filling every cell; candidate panels are mostly flat fill with sparse ornament. (5) Shadow — peers show consistent drop/inner shadows; candidate shows minimal to no shadowing. The golden urn ornaments and corner rosettes have competent pixel craft but still in a warm heraldic register alien to the Denzi dungeon palette. Charm dimension: peers are utilitarian-grim, not cute-Echo register; candidate is decorative-elegant but still not Echo-cute — neutral/no penalty here. Overall: too many style-axis failures across color, outline, density, and shadow to pass even allowing for UI-panel item-type latitude.

Candidate (right panel): golden-bordered dialog box with parchment interior — warm, Zelda-adjacent, broadly on-tone for a Nintendo family-friendly fairy-tale target. No grimdark, no fan-service, no cold-corporate treatment in the candidate itself. However, the peer reference assets (left tileset) are a significant red flag: heavy grey stone dungeon tiles, red blood stains, dark teal portals, skull/bone debris, and fire-lava elements — a textbook desaturated dungeon-crawler palette that directly violates the bright/warm/whimsical target. This peer context signals systemic tonal drift in the broader asset library even if the dialog panel survives on its own merits. The candidate panel is also somewhat generic (plain dual-rectangle layout, standard RPG gold frame) — it passes the 'not grimdark' bar but lacks the toy-diorama warmth and distinctive fairy-tale charm (no organic curves, no playful motifs, no hand-crafted softness) needed to firmly anchor the Echoes/Hyrule target aesthetic. Score held at marginal pending asset library audit.
